user_query:
Read these files first: input/project_requirements.json, input/endpoints.csv, and input/constraints.txt. Based on that project context, select the most appropriate backend architecture pattern and build a minimal, testable Python scaffold that reflects your choice. Then write the following artifacts under output/:

1) output/decision.json — A concise decision record with fields:
- chosen_pattern: one of ["clean architecture", "hexagonal", "ports & adapters", "domain-driven design"]
- decision_reason: 2-4 sentences explaining why this pattern fits the provided requirements, explicitly referencing the presence of multiple external integrations and testability/adapter swap concerns if applicable
- inputs_digest: a JSON object capturing key factors from the input files you used to make the decision (e.g., team size, integrations, volatility of providers, domain complexity)

2) output/structure.txt — A textual tree (one path per line) of the scaffold you created under output/src/. Include at minimum these directories (with trailing slashes) and files:
- output/src/
- output/src/domain/
- output/src/domain/entities/
- output/src/domain/services/
- output/src/ports/
- output/src/ports/inbound/
- output/src/ports/outbound/
- output/src/adapters/
- output/src/adapters/primary/
- output/src/adapters/primary/cli/
- output/src/adapters/secondary/
- output/src/adapters/secondary/persistence/
- output/src/adapters/secondary/payment/
- output/src/adapters/secondary/notification/
- output/src/infrastructure/
- output/src/domain/entities/order.py
- output/src/domain/services/order_service.py
- output/src/ports/inbound/place_order.py
- output/src/ports/outbound/payment_gateway.py
- output/src/ports/outbound/notification.py
- output/src/ports/outbound/order_repository.py
- output/src/adapters/secondary/payment/mock_payment.py
- output/src/adapters/secondary/notification/console_notifier.py
- output/src/adapters/secondary/persistence/in_memory_order_repository.py
- output/src/adapters/primary/cli/place_order_cli.py
- output/src/infrastructure/composition_root.py

3) Create the Python files listed above with minimal stub implementations that demonstrate the pattern boundaries (standard library only, no external packages):
- output/src/domain/entities/order.py: define class Order with methods is_valid(self) -> bool and mark_as_paid(self) -> None
- output/src/ports/outbound/payment_gateway.py: define an abstract base class PaymentGatewayPort with abstract method charge(self, amount_cents: int, customer_id: str) -> dict
- output/src/ports/outbound/notification.py: define abstract base class NotificationPort with abstract method send(self, email: str, message: str) -> None
- output/src/ports/outbound/order_repository.py: define abstract base class OrderRepositoryPort with abstract method save(self, order: "Order") -> "Order"
- output/src/ports/inbound/place_order.py: define an abstract base class PlaceOrderPort with abstract method execute(self, order: "Order") -> dict
- output/src/domain/services/order_service.py: define class OrderService implementing PlaceOrderPort.execute and depending only on the outbound port interfaces (PaymentGatewayPort, OrderRepositoryPort, NotificationPort). Keep domain code free of any adapter or framework imports.
- output/src/adapters/secondary/payment/mock_payment.py: define class MockPaymentAdapter(PaymentGatewayPort) with a concrete charge implementation that returns a dict like {"success": True, "transaction_id": "mock-123"}
- output/src/adapters/secondary/notification/console_notifier.py: define class ConsoleNotifierAdapter(NotificationPort) with a concrete send implementation (e.g., a simple print or pass)
- output/src/adapters/secondary/persistence/in_memory_order_repository.py: define class InMemoryOrderRepositoryAdapter(OrderRepositoryPort) with a concrete save that stores orders in memory and returns the saved order
- output/src/adapters/primary/cli/place_order_cli.py: a minimal CLI-style driving adapter that accepts an Order object and calls PlaceOrderPort.execute
- output/src/infrastructure/composition_root.py: wire together OrderService with the mock adapters, showing how adapters are composed around ports (e.g., build an OrderService using InMemoryOrderRepositoryAdapter, MockPaymentAdapter, ConsoleNotifierAdapter). This file can import adapters and ports to assemble the object graph.

Rules:
- Maintain clear boundaries: domain and ports must not import from adapters. Keep business logic independent of frameworks or databases.
- Use only relative workspace paths and write all created files under output/.
- Make the code self-contained stubs (no actual I/O beyond simple prints in adapters if desired).

Deliver exactly and only the files above under output/.
